title: User, Agent, and Tool Authority, Least Agency, Dry Runs, and Revocation · Language Clinic: Authority Principals
summary: The model inherits a service account, treats user intent as permission, uses write tools for read tasks, and cannot distinguish allowed, approval-required, or denied actions. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the authority principals increment in least-agency authorization decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
Reject unknown, disabled, stale, unauthorized, and version-mismatched tools without dynamic resolution or side effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Authority Principals, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-agency authorization decision engine.

content.code:
# Worked focus: agentpy-authority-principals — Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-tool-authorization-policy-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-tool-authorization-policy-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_tool_authorization_policy_3(requested_actions: int = 24, authorized_actions: int = 10, denied_actions: int = 8, approval_actions: int = 6, privilege_leaks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if requested_actions < 1 or authorized_actions < 0 or denied_actions < 0:
        return False, requested_actions, "authorization-count"
    if authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions != requested_actions:
        return False, authorized_actions, "authorization-reconciliation"
    if approval_actions < 1:
        return False, approval_actions, "approval-boundary"
    if privilege_leaks > 0:
        return False, privilege_leaks, "privilege-leak"
    assert authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions == requested_actions and privilege_leaks == 0
    return True, authorized_actions, "least-agency-policy"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python agent decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected model, interaction, schema, tool, authority, state, approval, memory, evaluation, security, telemetry, sandbox, deployment, or recovery case, then state which provider, Gemini, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, identity, storage, browser, accessibility, privacy, load, cost, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.
